cancer.name: Ewing Sarcoma
cancer.sub: A rare, aggressive bone and soft tissue sarcoma of children, adolescents, and young adults, defined by a single recurrent chimeric transcription factor, most often EWSR1-FLI1 (less commonly EWSR1-ERG). Cure of localized disease rests on intensive multiagent chemotherapy plus local control, but relapsed and metastatic disease has few dedicated options, so this board maps therapies borrowed from the same fusion driven, replication stressed, and surface antigen defined biology.
chips: EWSR1-FLI1 fusion (or EWSR1-ERG), the driver transcription factor
Replication stress and a BRCA deficient like phenotype
VDC/IE chemotherapy with local control is standard of care

voiceNote.clinician: Precise terms, fusion biology and trial outcomes stated.
voiceNote.researcher: Mechanism and cross condition repurposing rationale foregrounded.

overview.patient:
Ewing sarcoma is a rare cancer that usually starts in a bone, or sometimes the soft tissue near a bone, most often in children, teenagers, and young adults. Almost every case is caused by the same single genetic accident, where two chromosomes swap pieces and join two genes together into one abnormal gene. That fused gene makes an abnormal protein that switches on the wrong genetic program and drives the tumor to grow.

Treatment for disease that has not spread cures a majority of patients, but it is hard, combining several chemotherapy drugs with surgery, radiation, or both. When the cancer has spread or comes back, options are limited and rarely get their own trials. Doctors then look to treatments proven in cancers that share the same biology. This report maps out those borrowed options and the biology behind them.
overview.clinician:
Ewing sarcoma is a small round cell sarcoma of bone and soft tissue that peaks in the second decade of life. It is defined by a FET-ETS gene fusion, most commonly EWSR1-FLI1 from t(11;22)(q24;q12) and less commonly EWSR1-ERG, which encodes an aberrant transcription factor that is the initiating and sustaining oncogenic driver. Diagnosis rests on morphology, membranous CD99 staining, and molecular confirmation of the fusion.

Standard of care for localized disease in North America is interval compressed multiagent chemotherapy alternating vincristine, doxorubicin, and cyclophosphamide with ifosfamide and etoposide (VDC/IE), integrated with surgery and/or radiation for local control; the randomized Children's Oncology Group AEWS0031 trial established every 2 week dosing as more effective than every 3 week dosing without added toxicity. Outcomes for metastatic and relapsed disease remain poor, and no molecularly targeted agent is yet approved specifically for Ewing sarcoma. This cross condition analysis therefore indexes the fusion driven, DNA repair, and surface antigen biology to therapies validated elsewhere.
overview.researcher:
Ewing sarcoma is the archetypal fusion transcription factor sarcoma. The EWSR1-FLI1 chimera fuses the low complexity transactivation domain of EWSR1 to the ETS DNA binding domain of FLI1, generating a neomorphic transcription factor that binds GGAA microsatellites, remodels chromatin through recruited cofactors, and reprograms transcription. Beyond its transcriptional output, EWSR1-FLI1 imposes a distinctive DNA repair vulnerability: it increases damage induced transcription, provokes R-loop accumulation and replication stress, and impairs homologous recombination by sequestering BRCA1 at the elongating transcription machinery, yielding a BRCA deficient like state that underlies the marked preclinical sensitivity of Ewing cells to PARP inhibition and genotoxic chemotherapy.

These features define the actionable biology along three axes: the fusion transcription factor itself and its chromatin cofactors (for example LSD1), the replication stress and BRCAness that license DNA damage response combinations, and a set of selectively expressed cell surface antigens (CD99, STEAP1, IL1RAP, ENPP1) suited to antigen directed immunotherapy. The therapeutic opportunity lies in borrowing agents validated against these dependencies in other conditions, grading the transferability of each analogy, and in fusion directed strategies that remain investigational.

targetsIntro.patient: These are the biological features of Ewing sarcoma that drugs can target. Nearly all of them trace back to the one abnormal fusion gene that defines this cancer, which switches on the wrong program, stresses the tumor's DNA copying machinery, and puts unusual proteins on the cell surface.
targetsIntro.clinician: Actionable biology in Ewing sarcoma derives from the EWSR1-FLI1 (or EWSR1-ERG) fusion and its consequences: an aberrant transcriptional program with druggable chromatin cofactors, a replication stress and BRCA deficient like phenotype that sensitizes to DNA damage response agents, and selectively expressed surface antigens amenable to immunotherapy. Fusion confirmation and CD99 staining anchor the diagnosis.
targetsIntro.researcher: The single molecular dependency, the FET-ETS chimeric transcription factor, supports one still investigational upstream axis (the fusion and its cofactors), one DNA repair axis (replication stress and BRCAness), and one surface antigen axis for antigen directed therapy. Each target below anchors one or more translations in the next section.

targets.name: EWSR1-FLI1 fusion transcription factor (also EWSR1-ERG)
targets.present_in: Near universal in Ewing sarcoma (the defining lesion, roughly 85 percent EWSR1-FLI1)
targets.evidence: The t(11;22)(q24;q12) translocation fuses EWSR1 to the ETS DNA binding domain of FLI1, creating a chimeric transcription factor that is the diagnostic and pathogenic hallmark of Ewing sarcoma; EWSR1-ERG is the most common variant fusion (Delattre et al., Nature 1992, PMID 1522903, DOI 10.1038/359162a0).

targets.name: Replication stress and BRCA deficient like phenotype
targets.present_in: Ewing sarcoma broadly (fusion induced R-loops and homologous recombination impairment)
targets.evidence: EWSR1-FLI1 increases damage induced transcription, causes R-loop accumulation and replication stress, and impairs homologous recombination by enriching a BRCA1 interaction with the elongating transcription machinery, defining a BRCA deficient like state that explains sensitivity to genotoxic chemotherapy and PARP inhibitors (Gorthi et al., Nature 2018, PMID 29513652, DOI 10.1038/nature25748).

targets.name: PARP1 (DNA damage response dependency)
targets.present_in: Ewing sarcoma cell lines and xenografts (fusion coupled PARP1 dependence)
targets.evidence: EWSR1-FLI1 and EWSR1-ERG interact with PARP1 in a positive feedback loop required for fusion mediated transcription, and Ewing cells, xenografts, and metastases are highly sensitive to PARP1 inhibition, with the strongest activity seen in combination with the DNA damaging agent temozolomide (Brenner et al., Cancer Res 2012, PMID 22287547, DOI 10.1158/0008-5472.CAN-11-3648).

targets.name: LSD1 (KDM1A) chromatin cofactor
targets.present_in: Ewing sarcoma (EWSR1-FLI1 recruited coregulator, high LSD1 expression)
targets.evidence: EWSR1-FLI1 recruits the histone demethylase LSD1 within repressive chromatin remodeling complexes to enact its transcriptional program, and LSD1 blockade globally disrupts EWS-ETS function, providing a rationale for targeting the fusion indirectly through its cofactor (Theisen et al., Oncotarget 2016, PMID 26848860, DOI 10.18632/oncotarget.7124).

targets.name: STEAP1 and other Ewing surface antigens (CD99, IL1RAP, ENPP1)
targets.present_in: Ewing sarcoma (selectively expressed cell surface proteins)
targets.evidence: Proteomic surfaceome profiling of Ewing sarcoma xenografts confirms selective expression of STEAP1, CD99, IL1RAP, and ENPP1, nominating them as antigens for antibody, bispecific, and cell therapy approaches (Mooney et al., Clin Cancer Res 2024, PMID 37812652, DOI 10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-23-2187).

targets.name: IGF-1R signaling
targets.present_in: Ewing sarcoma (recurrent but not universal dependency)
targets.evidence: Type 1 insulin like growth factor receptor signaling is implicated in Ewing sarcoma pathogenesis and was the basis for IGF-1R antibody trials that produced documented but infrequent objective responses (Tap et al., J Clin Oncol 2012, PMID 22508822, DOI 10.1200/JCO.2011.37.2359).

therapiesIntro.patient: Each card is a treatment relevant to Ewing sarcoma, colored by how strong the evidence is. Green means it is the established standard of care for this disease. Amber means it is approved elsewhere or has documented but limited activity reported in Ewing sarcoma. Gray means the idea is mechanistically reasonable but still experimental. We are honest where drugs looked promising in the lab but underperformed in patients.
therapiesIntro.clinician: Candidates are graded Tier 1 (established standard of care for Ewing sarcoma), Tier 2 (approved elsewhere or with documented clinical activity in Ewing sarcoma, including negative or low response trials reported honestly), or Tier 3 (preclinical or mechanistic rationale only). Selection is guided by fusion confirmation, disease extent, and the DNA repair and surface antigen biology.
therapiesIntro.researcher: Translations are ranked by transferability of evidence rather than target novelty. Note the concentration on the fusion driven transcriptional program, the replication stress and BRCAness axis borrowed from BRCA deficient tumors, and selectively expressed surface antigens borrowed from immuno oncology. Several single agent targeted trials underperformed their preclinical promise, which the cards state explicitly.

therapies.drug: Vincristine, doxorubicin, cyclophosphamide alternating with ifosfamide and etoposide (VDC/IE)
therapies.approved_in: Standard of care for Ewing sarcoma with local control (surgery and/or radiation)
therapies.dev_stage: Established standard of care, randomized level 1 evidence

rationale.patient: This is the standard chemotherapy for Ewing sarcoma. It combines several drugs given in alternating cycles, together with surgery, radiation, or both to treat the original tumor. A large trial showed that giving the cycles every 2 weeks instead of every 3 weeks worked better without causing more side effects, and it cures a majority of patients whose disease has not spread.
rationale.clinician: Multiagent chemotherapy alternating vincristine, doxorubicin, and cyclophosphamide (VDC) with ifosfamide and etoposide (IE), integrated with surgery and/or radiation for local control, is the backbone of curative therapy for localized Ewing sarcoma. The randomized Children's Oncology Group AEWS0031 trial (NCT00006734) showed that interval compression to every 2 week dosing improved 5 year event free survival to 73 percent versus 65 percent for every 3 week dosing, with similar toxicity, establishing dose dense VDC/IE as standard. Outcomes for metastatic disease remain substantially worse.
rationale.researcher: The curative regimen exploits the intrinsic chemosensitivity of Ewing sarcoma, mechanistically consistent with the fusion induced replication stress and BRCA deficient like state that renders the tumor vulnerable to DNA damaging agents such as etoposide. AEWS0031 defines the standard against which all borrowed and novel strategies are benchmarked, and its dose density logic motivates combining DNA damage response inhibitors with this backbone.

citation.label: Womer et al., J Clin Oncol 2012 (AEWS0031, randomized interval compressed VDC/IE in localized Ewing sarcoma)

therapies.approved_in: Approved for BRCA mutated ovarian, breast, pancreatic, and prostate cancer; investigational in Ewing sarcoma
therapies.dev_stage: FDA approved elsewhere; single agent phase 2 in Ewing sarcoma showed no objective responses

rationale.patient: The Ewing fusion stresses the tumor's DNA copying machinery and weakens its DNA repair, which in the laboratory made the cells very sensitive to a class of drugs called PARP inhibitors. Olaparib is an approved PARP inhibitor pill for cancers with faulty DNA repair. Honestly, when olaparib was tried by itself in patients with advanced Ewing sarcoma, it did not shrink tumors, so the current interest is in combining it with DNA damaging chemotherapy rather than using it alone.
rationale.clinician: Oral PARP inhibitor approved for BRCA mutated ovarian, breast, pancreatic, and prostate cancers. Preclinical work showed Ewing cell lines harboring EWSR1-FLI1 are markedly sensitive to PARP inhibition, with strongest effect in combination with temozolomide. However, the dedicated phase 2 trial of single agent olaparib in relapsed or refractory Ewing sarcoma (NCT01583543) produced no objective responses among 12 evaluable patients (best response stable disease, median time to progression 5.7 weeks), so single agent activity is limited. Combination strategies with DNA damaging chemotherapy remain under investigation.
rationale.researcher: Olaparib operationalizes the fusion induced BRCAness and replication stress dependency, a direct borrow from BRCA deficient tumors. The dissociation between strong preclinical single agent activity and a negative single agent clinical trial illustrates the transferability gap: the vulnerability is real but likely requires combination with a DNA damaging partner (temozolomide, alkylators, or the standard etoposide containing backbone) and possibly pharmacodynamic patient selection, which the discovery layer develops.

citation.label: Choy et al., BMC Cancer 2014 (phase 2 olaparib in refractory Ewing sarcoma, no objective responses)

therapies.approved_in: Investigational; documented but infrequent activity in Ewing family tumors
therapies.dev_stage: Phase 2 in metastatic Ewing family tumors, low but real response rate

rationale.patient: Ewing sarcoma cells often rely on a growth signal called IGF-1R. Ganitumab is an antibody that blocks this signal. In a trial of patients with advanced Ewing family tumors, a small number of patients had their tumors shrink, so it has real but infrequent activity. This is an honest example of a treatment that helps some patients but not most.
rationale.clinician: Fully human anti IGF-1R monoclonal antibody. In the phase 2 trial in metastatic Ewing family tumors or desmoplastic small round cell tumors, ganitumab monotherapy produced an objective response rate of 6 percent (2 of 35 evaluable) with 49 percent stable disease and a clinical benefit rate of 17 percent, and was well tolerated. The magnitude confirms that IGF-1R directed antibodies have documented but infrequent single agent activity, without a validated predictive biomarker.
rationale.researcher: Ganitumab tests the IGF-1R dependency as a same target borrow from other IGF-1R directed programs. The reproducible but low response rate, with no relationship to fusion status or IGF-1 levels, marks IGF-1R as a real but incompletely penetrant dependency, motivating combination and biomarker driven approaches rather than single agent use.

citation.label: Tap et al., J Clin Oncol 2012 (phase 2 ganitumab in metastatic Ewing family tumors)

therapies.approved_in: Investigational; limited activity across sarcomas including Ewing
therapies.dev_stage: Phase 2 in recurrent or refractory sarcomas, limited activity

rationale.patient: R1507 is another antibody that blocks the IGF-1R growth signal. In a sarcoma trial it was safe but shrank tumors in only a small fraction of patients, reinforcing that blocking this signal alone is not enough for most people with these cancers.
rationale.clinician: Monoclonal anti IGF-1R antibody. In the SARC phase 2 study across recurrent or refractory bone and soft tissue sarcomas, R1507 was safe and well tolerated but had limited activity, with an overall objective response rate of 2.5 percent, underscoring that single agent IGF-1R blockade yields only occasional responses without predictive selection. It is presented here as a second, corroborating IGF-1R data point rather than an effective single agent.
rationale.researcher: R1507 provides an independent replication of the IGF-1R borrow and its ceiling. Together with ganitumab, it establishes that IGF-1R is a genuine but low frequency dependency in Ewing sarcoma, and that the modality requires either combination, biased agonism aware engineering, or biomarker enrichment to be useful.

citation.label: Pappo et al., Cancer 2014 (SARC phase 2 R1507 anti IGF-1R antibody in recurrent or refractory sarcomas)

therapies.dev_stage: Phase 1 (NCT03600649); preclinical reversal of fusion transcription

rationale.patient: Because the Ewing fusion protein itself is very hard to block directly, one strategy is to block a helper protein it depends on, called LSD1. Seclidemstat is an experimental pill that targets LSD1. In the laboratory it reverses the abnormal genetic program driven by the fusion, and it has been tested in an early clinical trial for Ewing and related sarcomas. It is still experimental and unlikely to work by itself, so it is being studied in combination.
rationale.clinician: Reversible LSD1 inhibitor developed for FET rearranged sarcomas. Preclinically, seclidemstat and its analog SP-2509 reverse the EWSR1-FLI1 transcriptional signature and are cytotoxic across FET fusion positive sarcoma cell lines including Ewing sarcoma, desmoplastic small round cell tumor, clear cell sarcoma, and myxoid liposarcoma. It entered a first in human phase 1 trial in Ewing sarcoma and other FET rearranged sarcomas (NCT03600649). Investigators note that such epigenetic inhibitors are unlikely to be effective as single agents and are being developed in combination.
rationale.researcher: Seclidemstat operationalizes the cofactor strategy for an otherwise undruggable fusion transcription factor, targeting the LSD1 coregulator that EWSR1-FLI1 recruits. It anchors the indirect fusion targeting axis and, given the expected need for combination, connects directly to the discovery layer hypothesis of pairing fusion or cofactor blockade with DNA damage response therapy.

citation.label: Rask et al., Cancer Res Commun 2025 (seclidemstat reverses FET fusion transcription in Ewing and related sarcomas, NCT03600649)

therapies.dev_stage: Preclinical, potent activity in Ewing xenografts

rationale.patient: Ewing sarcoma cells carry a protein called STEAP1 on their surface. Experimental immune therapies can be built to grab STEAP1 and pull immune T cells onto the tumor to kill it. In laboratory and animal studies this approach was highly effective against Ewing sarcoma, but it has not yet been proven in patients.
rationale.clinician: A STEAP1 by CD3 bispecific T cell engager redirects T cells against STEAP1 expressing tumors. In preclinical models it induced potent cytotoxicity against Ewing family tumor and other cell lines despite low antigen density, drove marked tumor infiltrating lymphocyte recruitment, and controlled cell line and patient derived Ewing xenografts. STEAP1 is confirmed as a selectively expressed Ewing surface antigen by proteomic surfaceome profiling. This is a preclinical surface antigen borrow, not yet clinically validated in Ewing sarcoma.
rationale.researcher: The STEAP1 engager exemplifies the surface antigen axis, borrowing T cell redirection from prostate cancer and other STEAP1 expressing tumors. Efficacy at low antigen density is encouraging for a tumor whose surface antigens are shared with normal tissues, and it sets up the discovery hypothesis of driver informed cell therapy against Ewing surface or fusion neoantigens.

citation.label: Lin et al., J Immunother Cancer 2021 (anti STEAP1 bispecific T cell engager active against Ewing family tumors)

trials.title: Seclidemstat (SP-2577) in relapsed or refractory Ewing sarcoma and FET rearranged sarcomas
trials.eligibility: Relapsed or refractory Ewing sarcoma and other FET rearranged sarcomas

precedentIntro.patient: These are landmark moments that made treating this cancer by its specific biology a real approach. They are why borrowing a treatment idea proven against the same biology in another cancer is legitimate rather than speculative, and why the standard chemotherapy schedule is what it is.
precedentIntro.clinician: The discoveries and pivotal trials below establish the mechanistic and regulatory precedent for biology driven therapy in Ewing sarcoma: the defining fusion, the dose dense chemotherapy standard, the BRCA deficient like DNA repair vulnerability, and the surface antigen landscape.
precedentIntro.researcher: Each landmark validates one axis of Ewing biology: the FET-ETS fusion as the driver, the replication stress and BRCAness that license DNA damage response borrowing, the randomized chemotherapy standard, and the selectively expressed surface antigens. Together they convert an orphan sarcoma into a set of mechanism defined, trial eligible states.

precedents.biomarker: EWSR1-FLI1 fusion identification in Ewing sarcoma (fusion biology)
precedents.drug: molecular definition of Ewing sarcoma (t(11;22), EWSR1-FLI1)

citation.label: Delattre et al., Nature 1992 (EWSR1-FLI1 fusion defines Ewing sarcoma)

precedents.biomarker: Chemosensitivity of localized Ewing sarcoma (dose dense chemotherapy)
precedents.drug: interval compressed VDC/IE chemotherapy with local control

citation.label: Womer et al., J Clin Oncol 2012 (AEWS0031, randomized interval compression standard of care)

precedents.biomarker: EWSR1-FLI1 associated PARP sensitivity in Ewing cells (DNA repair vulnerability)

citation.label: Garnett et al., Nature 2012 (EWS-FLI1 Ewing cells markedly sensitive to PARP inhibitors)

precedents.biomarker: Replication stress and BRCA deficient like phenotype in Ewing sarcoma (BRCAness)
precedents.drug: genotoxic chemotherapy and PARP inhibition (mechanistic basis)

citation.label: Gorthi et al., Nature 2018 (EWS-FLI1 causes R-loops, replication stress, and impaired homologous recombination)

precedents.biomarker: Selectively expressed Ewing surface antigens (immunotherapeutic targets)
precedents.drug: surface antigen directed immunotherapy (STEAP1, CD99, IL1RAP, ENPP1)

citation.label: Mooney et al., Clin Cancer Res 2024 (Ewing sarcoma surfaceome nominates actionable immunotherapy targets)

discoveryIntro.patient: This section lists research ideas, not treatment recommendations. Because Ewing sarcoma is driven by one clear fusion gene and has a well understood weakness in DNA repair and unusual surface proteins, scientists can reason about new kinds of treatment worth testing. These are starting points for research, not advice for patients.
discoveryIntro.clinician: Each entry is a research hypothesis that transfers a strategy validated against a shared dependency, the fusion transcription factor and its cofactors, the replication stress and BRCAness axis, or a Ewing surface antigen, into a rationally designed approach. Transferability is graded and every analogy carries a primary citation. None is a clinical recommendation.
discoveryIntro.researcher: The discovery layer proposes cross condition hypotheses by analogy over the EWSR1-FLI1 dependency and its downstream axes, with transferability grading, a supporting citation for the analogy, and a falsifiable prediction. These are computationally generated research directions, not validated in Ewing sarcoma.

discovery.hypothesis: Research hypothesis (not a clinical recommendation): directly disable the EWSR1-FLI1 fusion transcription factor, by targeted degradation or by blocking its essential chromatin cofactors, rather than only inhibiting downstream signaling, testing whether attacking the driver itself is more durable than attacking its effectors.
discovery.shared: A single fusion transcription factor that is the initiating and sustaining driver of the disease, recruiting cofactors such as LSD1 to enact its program, analogous to other fusion transcription factor sarcomas where the chimeric protein reprograms transcription and chromatin.
discovery.source: Fusion transcription factor sarcomas (EWSR1-FLI1 as the defining driver)
discovery.rationale: IGF-1R and other downstream blockade have proven largely insufficient as single agents in Ewing sarcoma, and the fusion transcription factor is the apex dependency but historically undruggable directly. Strategies that degrade the fusion, block validated cofactors such as LSD1, or interrupt the specific GGAA microsatellite transcriptional program could be more durable, mirroring efforts against other oncogenic fusion transcription factors and building on preclinical LSD1 inhibition that reverses the EWSR1-FLI1 signature.
discovery.transferability: speculative, transcription factors are historically hard to drug directly, so success likely depends on targeted degradation chemistry or on a druggable cofactor, chromatin, or synthetic lethal dependency of the fusion, and early cofactor inhibitors are expected to need combination.
discovery.prediction: In Ewing sarcoma cells and patient derived models, selective degradation of EWSR1-FLI1 or inhibition of a validated fusion specific cofactor collapses the GGAA microsatellite transcriptional program and impairs viability more durably than IGF-1R or other downstream inhibition alone, without loss of activity on rescue of the fusion program.

citation.label: Theisen et al., Oncotarget 2016 (LSD1 as an EWS-FLI cofactor and indirect fusion targeting strategy)

discovery.hypothesis: Research hypothesis (not a clinical recommendation): combine PARP inhibition with DNA damaging chemotherapy or other DNA damage response inhibitors in Ewing sarcoma to exploit the fusion induced replication stress and BRCA deficient like state, testing whether combination overcomes the failure of single agent PARP inhibition.
discovery.shared: A BRCA deficient like phenotype with R-loop driven replication stress and impaired homologous recombination, the same DNA repair vulnerability that makes BRCA mutated tumors sensitive to PARP inhibitors and to synthetic lethal DNA damage response combinations.
discovery.source: BRCA deficient tumors and PARP inhibitor combinations (replication stress and BRCAness in Ewing sarcoma)
discovery.rationale: Ewing cells are markedly sensitive to PARP inhibition preclinically, with strongest activity alongside the DNA damaging agent temozolomide, yet single agent olaparib produced no objective responses in patients. The fusion induced replication stress, R-loop accumulation, and homologous recombination defect predict that PARP inhibition paired with a DNA damaging partner (temozolomide, alkylators, topoisomerase inhibitors) or with an ATR or WEE1 inhibitor that intensifies replication stress could convert preclinical vulnerability into clinical activity, transferring combination logic from BRCA deficient cancers.
discovery.transferability: medium, the DNA repair vulnerability is validated mechanistically and PARP inhibitors are approved elsewhere, but the negative single agent trial shows that the correct partner, schedule, and tolerability of stacking DNA damage must be established prospectively, with attention to hematologic toxicity.
discovery.prediction: In Ewing sarcoma models and a biomarker informed trial, a PARP inhibitor combined with a DNA damaging agent or a replication stress amplifying DNA damage response inhibitor yields greater tumor regression and longer progression free survival than single agent PARP inhibition, with pharmacodynamic evidence of increased unresolved DNA damage.

citation.label: Gorthi et al., Nature 2018 (replication stress and BRCA deficient like state underlying Ewing chemosensitivity and PARP rationale)

discovery.hypothesis: Research hypothesis (not a clinical recommendation): develop cell therapy directed against a Ewing surface antigen such as STEAP1, or against an EWSR1-FLI1 fusion derived neoantigen, testing whether engineered T cell approaches can provide a driver specific or lineage specific attack in a tumor with selectively expressed surface targets.
discovery.shared: A set of selectively expressed cell surface antigens (STEAP1, CD99, IL1RAP, ENPP1) plus a clonal, tumor specific fusion breakpoint that in principle yields a shared public neoantigen, a combination well suited to antigen directed cell therapy.
discovery.source: Adoptive and engineered cell therapy in antigen defined tumors (Ewing surfaceome and STEAP1)
discovery.rationale: Proteomic surfaceome profiling confirms Ewing sarcoma expresses STEAP1, CD99, IL1RAP, and ENPP1 selectively, and a STEAP1 bispecific T cell engager already shows potent preclinical activity against Ewing xenografts despite low antigen density. Because every Ewing sarcoma shares the EWSR1-FLI1 breakpoint and a defined lineage program, a CAR T cell, engineered TCR, or bispecific against a Ewing surface antigen or a fusion junction neoantigen could provide a driver specific or lineage specific attack, extending antigen directed cell therapy from other surface antigen defined tumors.
discovery.transferability: speculative, cell therapy against solid tumors remains early, and this approach requires a truly tumor restricted and immunogenic surface or fusion antigen, confirmation of HLA presentation for a neoantigen approach, and solutions for solid tumor trafficking, persistence, and antigen density.
discovery.prediction: Engineered T cells or a bispecific targeting a validated Ewing surface antigen or an EWSR1-FLI1 junction peptide selectively kill Ewing sarcoma cells ex vivo and control patient derived Ewing xenografts without cross reactivity against essential normal tissues.

citation.label: Mooney et al., Clin Cancer Res 2024 (Ewing sarcoma surfaceome including STEAP1 as actionable immunotherapeutic targets)

disclaimer: This dossier is a research and educational evidence summary compiled from PubMed and public regulatory and trial announcements. It is not medical advice and must not be used to guide individual patient care. Ewing sarcoma is a rare disease, and this report is included to demonstrate that Anveshar's biology first, cross condition matching framework applies to an orphan fusion driven pediatric and young adult sarcoma. The curative standard of care is intensive multiagent chemotherapy (interval compressed VDC/IE) integrated with surgery and/or radiation for local control, described from the randomized AEWS0031 trial. The targeted and immunotherapeutic agents shown are, except where stated, not approved specifically for Ewing sarcoma; several looked promising preclinically but showed limited single agent activity in patients (single agent olaparib produced no objective responses, and IGF-1R antibodies produced documented but infrequent responses), which is stated honestly. Surface antigen directed cell therapy and LSD1 cofactor inhibition are investigational or preclinical. Recruiting status of any trial referenced was not independently verified against ClinicalTrials.gov at the time of writing and must be confirmed before use. The discovery section lists computationally generated research hypotheses, explicitly not clinical recommendations, each an analogy over a shared molecular dependency with graded transferability. Treatment decisions belong to a qualified sarcoma and pediatric oncology team and a molecular tumor board, ideally at a sarcoma specialized center and, where relevant, within a clinical trial.
